title: Barking Cough - Croup (Laryngotracheobronchitis)
chief_complaint: 2-year-old with 2 days of runny nose and low-grade fever, now with barking cough, hoarse voice, and inspiratory stridor worse at night. No drooling.

physical_exam.chest: Inspiratory stridor at rest, mild subcostal retractions

hidden_data.cxr_soft_tissue: Steeple sign (subglottic narrowing) on AP neck X-ray

key_learning_points: Steeple sign on X-ray = subglottic narrowing

patient_responses.cough: It sounds like a seal barking
patient_responses.worse_at_night: Yes, much worse when he lies down

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Croup with classic barking cough.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is croup. The barking cough + stridor + steeple sign are diagnostic.
feedback.attending_note: Croup management: Dexamethasone 0.6mg/kg PO once (reduces severity and duration). Nebulized epinephrine if severe stridor at rest. Cool mist at home. Most cases are mild and self-limited. Parainfluenza is the most common cause.
